method: Web research against museum sites and listings press; dates verified against official or ticketing pages where possible. NYC, the DC-Baltimore-Philadelphia corridor, and Buffalo covered in depth via per-city venue checklists; Amsterdam swept across Museumkaart member museums. Access facts (museumkaart, free) recorded in the 'access' field. Closed/expired shows dropped (Bodies and Souls at PAFA, Renoir and Love at Orsay, Michelangelo/Rodin at the Louvre); date corrections made where venue pages had updated since the last refresh (Revolution! at the Met, Joan Miró at the Kreeger, Ana Mendieta at Tate Modern); new verified shows added to replace closures and deepen Amsterdam and Philadelphia coverage.

exhibitions.title: Whitney Biennial 2026
exhibitions.blurb: The 82nd edition gathers fifty-six artists, duos, and collectives across family, geopolitics, and myth — the museum's recurring temperature-reading of art in America.
exhibitions.note: Curated by Marcela Guerrero & Drew Sawyer

exhibitions.title: Carol Bove
exhibitions.blurb: Bove's first-ever museum survey fills the entire Frank Lloyd Wright rotunda — twenty-five years of crushed-steel 'collage sculptures' running in reverse chronology down the spiral.
exhibitions.note: Closing very soon — curated by Katherine Brinson

exhibitions.title: Revolution!
exhibitions.blurb: The Met's America-250 centerpiece — painting, sculpture, furniture, beadwork and silver tracing the Revolution's roots, course and aftermath across the whole collection.
exhibitions.note: American Wing and beyond; closing date corrected to Sep 7 per the Met's official page

exhibitions.title: Ideas of Africa: Portraiture and Political Imagination
exhibitions.blurb: A photography-led exploration of Pan-African subjectivity and solidarity, drawn largely from the 2019 Pigozzi gift of modern and contemporary African art.
exhibitions.note: Final days — closes 25 Jul

exhibitions.title: Greater New York 2026
exhibitions.blurb: PS1's once-every-five-years survey of art being made in the city right now — over 150 works spanning the entire building in Long Island City.
exhibitions.note: MoMA PS1 is free for all visitors through 2028

exhibitions.title: Sara Flores: Akinananti
exhibitions.blurb: The Shipibo-Konibo painter's kené patterns — an ancient Amazonian medium made with materials from the forest — in a rare New York solo.
exhibitions.note: Free gallery entry

exhibitions.title: Rembrandt's Lions: Art and Exile in the Dutch Republic
exhibitions.blurb: The Morgan gathers Rembrandt's celebrated lion drawings and the world of the Dutch Republic around them — intimate sheets by a master observer of living things.
exhibitions.note: Pairs well with the restored Morgan library itself

exhibitions.title: Paul Klee: Other Possible Worlds
exhibitions.blurb: The first American museum show devoted to Klee's powerful late work — some one hundred paintings and drawings from his last, unsettling decade.
exhibitions.note: Final weekend — closes 26 Jul

exhibitions.title: Modernity and Opulence: Women of the Wiener Werkstätte
exhibitions.blurb: For the first time, the lasting contributions of the Jewish women who shaped the Wiener Werkstätte's modernist design language, as artists and as patrons.
exhibitions.note: Just opened; organized with Vienna's MAK museum

exhibitions.title: Yves Saint Laurent and Photography
exhibitions.blurb: More than three hundred photographs and archival objects tracing four decades of Saint Laurent's dialogue with the camera — promotion, muse, and legacy-maker at once.
exhibitions.note: With Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris

exhibitions.title: Sophie Rivera: Double Exposures
exhibitions.blurb: The Nuyorican photographer's probing portraits and experiments in doubled imagery, reclaiming an under-sung figure of New York photography.
exhibitions.note: Closes 2 Aug

exhibitions.title: Anicka Yi: Message from the Mud
exhibitions.blurb: Yi's first large-scale outdoor project stages a fictional archaeological dig — twenty living Winogradsky columns culturing Storm King's own pond mud into slow paintings.
exhibitions.note: Storm King, New Windsor — Hudson Valley day trip

exhibitions.title: Marcel Duchamp
exhibitions.blurb: The first North American Duchamp retrospective in over fifty years, spanning six decades and every medium he touched — readymades, optics, and the unfinished Étant donnés among them.
exhibitions.note: Special exhibition ticket required; travels to the Philadelphia Museum of Art in October

exhibitions.title: Iris van Herpen: Sculpting the Senses
exhibitions.blurb: The North American debut of this retrospective gathers more than 140 haute couture creations by the Dutch designer alongside the scientific and artistic references that inspired them.
exhibitions.note: Separately ticketed, timed entry recommended

exhibitions.title: Ruffles and Ribbons: Fashion Plates from the Time of Marie Antoinette
exhibitions.artist: Various, after Gallerie des modes et costumes français
exhibitions.blurb: Two dozen hand-colored engravings of late-eighteenth-century French fashion, a small Cabinet installation shown alongside the Frick's Gainsborough.
exhibitions.note: Included with general admission; closing very soon

exhibitions.title: Noguchi's New York
exhibitions.blurb: Fifty-plus works and archival documents trace Noguchi's lifelong relationship with the city, marking the 40th anniversary of his foundation.
exhibitions.note: Free on Free First Fridays and select second Saturdays

exhibitions.title: Buddha and Shiva, Lotus and Dragon: Celebrating 70 Years of Asia Society and the Rockefeller Legacy
exhibitions.blurb: Seventy masterworks in bronze, ceramic, and metal assembled by John D. Rockefeller 3rd, marking Asia Society's 70th anniversary.
exhibitions.note: Museum is free on Fridays

exhibitions.title: The Seventh AIM Biennial: Forms of Connection
exhibitions.blurb: Artists from the museum's year-long Artist in the Marketplace fellowship show work organized around heritage, society, and community.
exhibitions.note: Museum admission is always free; run extended into September

exhibitions.title: Basquiat × Banksy
exhibitions.blurb: Basquiat's 'Boy and Dog in a Johnnypump' faces Banksy's answer, 'Banksquiat' — plus twenty early Basquiat works on paper and the film Downtown 81.
exhibitions.note: Free, like all Smithsonian museums; run extended through Sep 7

exhibitions.title: Dear America: Artists Explore the American Experience
exhibitions.blurb: 115 works on paper reflecting on the nature of the American experience — the National Gallery's quiet, thoughtful contribution to the 250th.
exhibitions.note: West Building; free entry

exhibitions.title: American Icon: The US Flag in Art
exhibitions.blurb: Thirty-plus works from the late 1800s to today tracing how artists have used, saluted and interrogated the flag.
exhibitions.note: East Building Concourse; free

exhibitions.title: Adam Pendleton: Love, Queen
exhibitions.blurb: New and recent paintings plus a video work wrapping the second-floor inner ring — Pendleton's first Washington solo, mounted for the museum's 50th anniversary.
exhibitions.note: On view into 2027

exhibitions.title: The Outwin 2025: American Portraiture Today
exhibitions.blurb: More than thirty portraits from the seventh Outwin Boochever competition — the country's running argument about what a portrait can be.
exhibitions.note: First floor galleries

exhibitions.title: Photographic Memory: Fifty Years of Photography at the National Portrait Gallery
exhibitions.blurb: A hundred-plus works from daguerreotype to digital, marking fifty years of the museum collecting the photographed face.
exhibitions.note: Semiquincentennial season

exhibitions.title: State Fairs: Growing American Craft
exhibitions.blurb: The first major exhibition on artists' contributions to the American state fair — blue-ribbon craft from quilts to butter sculpture, taken seriously at last.
exhibitions.note: Includes Justin Favela's piñata-corn Grand Salon

exhibitions.title: Nick Cave: Mammoth
exhibitions.blurb: A monumental new commission: visitors walk among the fantastical remains of ancient creatures, Cave's excavation of American memory at a fraught Smithsonian moment.
exhibitions.note: New commission for SAAM

exhibitions.title: Isaac Julien: Lessons of the Hour — Frederick Douglass
exhibitions.blurb: Julien's five-screen portrait of Frederick Douglass interweaves period reenactment with poetic image and sound — justice, abolition, and freedom in 28 minutes.
exhibitions.note: Final weeks of the long DC run

exhibitions.title: Here: Pride and Belonging in African Art
exhibitions.blurb: The largest-ever exhibition of LGBTQ+ African art — some sixty works by thirty artists, including Zanele Muholi and Rotimi Fani-Kayode.
exhibitions.note: Closing in August; free, like all Smithsonian museums

exhibitions.title: Of the Hills: Pahari Paintings from India's Himalayan Kingdoms
exhibitions.blurb: Forty-eight rare Pahari court paintings made for Hindu kings in India's Himalayan foothills between the 1620s and 1830s, many shown publicly for the first time.
exhibitions.note: Closes late July; free, like all Smithsonian museums

exhibitions.title: Making Their Mark: Works from the Shah Garg Collection
exhibitions.artist: Group show, curated by Cecilia Alemani
exhibitions.venue: National Museum of Women in the Arts
exhibitions.blurb: Some eighty works by seventy major contemporary artists — Julie Mehretu, Faith Ringgold, Joan Mitchell, and Kara Walker among them.
exhibitions.note: Ticketed general admission, not free; final week

exhibitions.title: Burnished: Pueblo Pottery
exhibitions.venue: National Museum of Women in the Arts
exhibitions.blurb: Two dozen clay vessels by Pueblo women potters of the American Southwest, shown alongside the museum's collection.
exhibitions.note: Ticketed general admission, not free

exhibitions.title: Joan Miró: From the Kreeger Collection
exhibitions.blurb: Miró works drawn from the Kreeger's own holdings, shown in the Potomac Palisades house-museum built for the family who assembled them.
exhibitions.note: Ticketed general admission, reservations recommended; end date corrected to Aug 15

exhibitions.title: Tomorrow the Sun Will Rise: The Phillips Collection Juried Exhibition
exhibitions.blurb: Forty-eight artists living and working in the greater Washington, DC region show together in America's first museum of modern art's juried exhibition.
exhibitions.note: Opens Aug 1

exhibitions.title: Fratino and Matisse: To See This Light Again
exhibitions.blurb: Louis Fratino's tender domestic scenes hung alongside the Matisses that shaped them — drawn from the world's largest public collection of Matisse's work.
exhibitions.note: One of a trio of 2026 Matisse shows

exhibitions.title: Matisse and Martinique: Portraits and Poetry
exhibitions.blurb: A focused chapter of the BMA's unrivalled Matisse holdings, pairing portraiture with poetry inspired by his 1930 Martinique visit.
exhibitions.note: Cone Collection country

exhibitions.title: Sondheim Art Prize Finalists 2026
exhibitions.artist: Thea Canlas, Leigh Davis, Brandon Donahue-Shipp, Curran Hatleberg, Danni O'Brien
exhibitions.blurb: Finalists for the Janet & Walter Sondheim Art Prize, Baltimore's flagship award for working artists, shown on Mount Vernon Place.
exhibitions.note: Free admission; winner announced Aug 27

exhibitions.title: Douriean Fletcher: Jewelry of the Afrofuture
exhibitions.blurb: The self-taught jeweler behind Black Panther's adornment — the first jeweler admitted to the Motion Picture Costumers Union — in a gleaming museum showcase.
exhibitions.note: Ticketed special exhibition, organized with the Museum of Arts and Design

exhibitions.title: Fantastic Realities
exhibitions.blurb: AVAM's 30th-anniversary mega-show: 130-plus works of depicted worlds — surreal oils, ceramic subway scenes, chainsaw-carved minotaurs, hand-painted galaxies.
exhibitions.note: Anniversary exhibition, whole main building

exhibitions.title: Raúl de Nieves: and imagine you are here
exhibitions.blurb: De Nieves' shimmering beaded figures and stained-glass-like panels transform the BMA's East Lobby into a processional fantasy, on long-term view.
exhibitions.note: East Lobby commission

exhibitions.title: The Active Power of Touch
exhibitions.artist: Group show, curated by Dr. Cheryl Fogle-Hatch
exhibitions.blurb: Twelve artists explore tactile, touchable art in clay, plaster, stone, and textile — designed to be experienced by blind and sighted visitors alike.
exhibitions.note: Touchable works, allow extra time

exhibitions.title: Let Them Speak: Their Beauty, Our Hearts
exhibitions.artist: Tyrone West, Xavier Hill, Donnell Rochester, Adiena Britt
exhibitions.blurb: Portraits, shoe art, fashion, and fine art from four Baltimore artists, organized with the West Coalition.
exhibitions.note: Free admission; closing this weekend

exhibitions.title: A Nation of Artists
exhibitions.blurb: A thousand-plus portraits, sculptures and photographs spanning three centuries of American art — a blockbuster split across two museums on the Parkway.
exhibitions.note: Dual-venue with PAFA; PAFA's run extends about two months longer

exhibitions.title: Fred Wilson: The Master Plan
exhibitions.blurb: Twenty-two photogravures reproducing museum floor-plan maps — a wry meditation on how institutions frame art, shown alongside A Nation of Artists.
exhibitions.note: Works on Paper Gallery, Historic Landmark Building

exhibitions.title: Freedom Dreams
exhibitions.blurb: Film, video and immersive installations by five Black artists — Arthur Jafa, Garrett Bradley, David Hartt, Ja'Tovia Gary and Tourmaline — on how Americans of color shaped the national story.
exhibitions.note: Roberts Gallery; plan for seated viewing time

exhibitions.title: Sky Hopinka: Red Metal Dust
exhibitions.blurb: Eleven immersive panels layering landscape photographs over copper sheets — a commissioned meditation on America's history with Indigenous peoples, filling the Annenberg Court.
exhibitions.note: Annenberg Court commission

exhibitions.title: A World in the Making: The Shakers
exhibitions.venue: Institute of Contemporary Art, University of Pennsylvania
exhibitions.blurb: Original Shaker furniture and objects alongside contemporary artists channeling — and challenging — the sect's radical design legacy of community, labor, and equality.
exhibitions.note: ICA is always free; organized with the Vitra Design Museum

exhibitions.title: Workshop of the World: Arts and Crafts in Philadelphia
exhibitions.blurb: Two hundred fifty-plus objects — ceramics, furniture, metalwork, stained glass — spotlighting Philadelphia's Arts and Crafts movement, including work by Samuel Yellin and Violet Oakley.
exhibitions.note: Opened July 12, timed to the city's 250th

exhibitions.title: Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments
exhibitions.blurb: Ties the fiftieth anniversary of Rocky to the history and meaning of public monuments in the city.
exhibitions.note: Closes early August

exhibitions.title: Jesse Krimes: Elegy Quilts
exhibitions.blurb: Quilts made from materials contributed by currently and formerly incarcerated people explore memories of home, shown with a new Mural Arts collaboration.
exhibitions.note: First Fabric Workshop and Museum entry in this tracker

exhibitions.title: Let Us Gather in a Flourishing Way
exhibitions.blurb: Contemporary Latinx artists pushing at the traditions of painting — a cross-generational survey of material and cultural innovation, before touring nationally.
exhibitions.note: Bilingual English/Spanish presentation

exhibitions.title: Ali Banisadr: Temple of the Mind
exhibitions.blurb: The AKG's first-ever artist collection intervention — Banisadr threads his own work through the permanent galleries alongside his picks from Hiroshige to Goya.
exhibitions.note: Hemicycle Gallery and throughout the Wilmers Building

exhibitions.title: Kindred: Works from the Burchfield Penney and Seneca-Iroquois National Museum Collections
exhibitions.blurb: A collaboration with the Seneca-Iroquois National Museum on how artists preserve, reinterpret, and evolve cultural practices across both collections.
exhibitions.note: On view at the Burchfield Penney Off Center at K Art, 808 Main Street

exhibitions.title: A VAST, tiny WORLD: Burchfield's Insects & Spiders
exhibitions.blurb: Burchfield's lifelong fascination with the insect world, from field-journal studies to the vibrating, haloed creatures of his late watercolors.
exhibitions.note: Curated by Burchfield scholar Nancy Weekly

exhibitions.title: Ed van der Elsken: Up Close
exhibitions.blurb: A sweeping retrospective drawing on the newly examined personal archive of one of the twentieth century's most beloved Dutch photographers.
exhibitions.note: Nine galleries trace his career from 1950s reportage to his final photobook

exhibitions.title: Fiep Westendorp
exhibitions.blurb: Around 150 original drawings celebrate the illustrator behind Jip en Janneke, Otje, and Pluk van de Petteflet.
exhibitions.note: Same building and dates as Ed van der Elsken

exhibitions.title: Carel Visser in the Rijksmuseum Gardens
exhibitions.blurb: Monumental steel sculptures by the Netherlands' foremost twentieth-century sculptor, gathered outdoors for the first time.
exhibitions.note: The gardens are free to visit even without a museum ticket

exhibitions.title: Vincent's Path to Fame
exhibitions.blurb: Traces how Vincent's brother Theo, sister-in-law Jo, and nephew Vincent built and safeguarded the collection that made him world-famous, featuring works including Almond Blossom and The Harvest.
exhibitions.note: Timed entry — book well ahead; shown for the final time this summer

exhibitions.title: Yayoi Kusama: Retrospective
exhibitions.blurb: Seven decades of dots, nets and mirror rooms in a full retrospective — Amsterdam's big autumn ticket, worth planning a trip around.
exhibitions.note: Opens 11 Sep — set a reminder

exhibitions.title: Danh Vo: πνεῦμα
exhibitions.blurb: Vo's choreography of collected objects, classical fragments, and personal history returns to the Stedelijk after his 2008 debut there.
exhibitions.note: Closes Aug 2 — visit soon

exhibitions.title: Beyond the Manosphere: Masculinities Today
exhibitions.blurb: Thirty-five artists across generations examine masculinity as performance, power, and contested identity, countering online 'manosphere' rhetoric.
exhibitions.note: Closes Aug 2, same day as Danh Vo — a good double bill

exhibitions.title: Kho Liang Ie: Mid-Century Modernist
exhibitions.blurb: The first major retrospective in over fifty years of the designer behind Schiphol's interiors and landmark Dutch furniture.
exhibitions.note: Includes a reconstruction of part of his 1971 Stedelijk exhibition

exhibitions.title: American Identities: David Levinthal & ChicanX
exhibitions.blurb: H'ART's America-focused year with the Smithsonian: Levinthal's staged toy tableaux alongside ChicanX portraiture, marking 250 years of Dutch-American ties.
exhibitions.note: First European collaboration with the Smithsonian American Art Museum

exhibitions.title: The Land of Chocolate Sprinkles: Building a New World Together
exhibitions.blurb: The Congolese collective CATPC debuts in Amsterdam with sculptures made from cocoa, palm oil, and sugar confronting plantation economics.
exhibitions.note: Proceeds from related sales help the artists buy back exhausted plantation land

exhibitions.title: Martin Parr: Very Modern and Rather Ugly
exhibitions.blurb: Five decades of Parr's saturated, deadpan chronicle of consumer culture and everyday ritual — his most iconic series in one landmark show.
exhibitions.note: Compact canal-house museum; closes soon

exhibitions.title: Foam Talent 2026
exhibitions.blurb: Fifteen artists chosen from nearly 3,000 submissions across 107 countries — Foam's annual scouting report on where photography is heading.
exhibitions.note: Same building as Martin Parr

exhibitions.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
exhibitions.blurb: Eleven artists respond to Eye's archive of some 2,000 colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname with ten newly commissioned works.
exhibitions.note: Across the IJ — free ferry from Centraal

exhibitions.title: Rembrandt & Life
exhibitions.blurb: More than fifty prints tracing the whole of life, cradle to grave — shown in the house where Rembrandt lived and worked.
exhibitions.note: In Rembrandt's actual house

exhibitions.title: Martine Gutierrez: Wunderkind
exhibitions.blurb: The American artist's shape-shifting self-portraiture takes over two seventeenth-century canal houses on the Keizersgracht.
exhibitions.note: Amsterdam's photography jewel box

exhibitions.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
exhibitions.blurb: The year's prize-winning photojournalism in the Nieuwe Kerk's Gothic nave — the world's news cycle slowed down to the strength of single images.
exhibitions.note: On Dam Square, beside the Palace

exhibitions.title: Made in China
exhibitions.blurb: What does the label really mean? Millennia of Chinese making — from imperial craft to contemporary art and design — unpacked at the Wereldmuseum.
exhibitions.note: Final month — closes 31 Aug

exhibitions.title: Larissa Sansour: Rogue Agents of History
exhibitions.blurb: The Palestinian artist's first Dutch solo show — film and installation on memory, myth-making, and speculative futures.
exhibitions.note: Same building as Made in China

exhibitions.title: Judy Chicago: Revelations
exhibitions.blurb: The first Dutch solo show of Judy Chicago — around 160 works tracing her feminist iconography and its ties to her Jewish heritage.
exhibitions.note: Closes late August

exhibitions.title: Diana Blok: Time Tells
exhibitions.blurb: A small side-gallery photography show on family history and memory, shown in the museum's Kunstkabinet.
exhibitions.note: Compact, pairs well with Judy Chicago

exhibitions.title: Ekō: Japan in Two Visual Narratives
exhibitions.artist: Anaïs López, with historical Japanese photography
exhibitions.blurb: Contemporary work by Anaïs López paired with historical Japanese photography from the museum's maritime archive, including rare 1859-era photo albums.
exhibitions.note: In the National Maritime Museum

exhibitions.title: Glass, Made in Antiquity
exhibitions.blurb: Over 200 ancient glass objects examined with nuclear-reactor and X-ray techniques, shown alongside two contemporary glass artists.
exhibitions.note: Extended run through autumn 2026

exhibitions.title: Ana Mendieta
exhibitions.blurb: Her first comprehensive UK exhibition in over a decade: over 150 works including the iconic Silueta series, newly remastered films, early paintings and late sculpture.
exhibitions.note: First UK survey in over a decade; confirmed dates run Jul 15 – Jan 17

exhibitions.title: Julio Le Parc: Light. Colour. Action.
exhibitions.blurb: Interactive installations, mirrored kinetic sculptures and op-art canvases from the 1950s to the 2010s — a posthumous retrospective, as Le Parc died days before it opened.
exhibitions.note: On view for nearly a year

exhibitions.title: Waldmüller: Landscapes
exhibitions.blurb: The first-ever UK exhibition of the Austrian Biedermeier master — alpine light rendered with near-photographic precision, drawn largely from Vienna's Belvedere.
exhibitions.note: Free admission, Room One

exhibitions.title: Richard Dadd: Beyond Bedlam
exhibitions.blurb: A survey of the Victorian painter of obsessive fairy miniatures, most made inside Bethlem and Broadmoor — strange, minute, unforgettable.
exhibitions.note: Just opened; book weekends ahead

exhibitions.title: Youssef Nabil: De rêver encore
exhibitions.blurb: The Egyptian photographer's hand-tinted, cinematic self-portraits and celebrity portraits, the first contemporary artist to occupy Orsay's Orientalist galleries.
exhibitions.note: On view through mid-September

exhibitions.title: Affinités: Les merveilles des arts de l'Islam aux Objets d'art
exhibitions.blurb: Around fifty Islamic-art objects from the ninth to seventeenth centuries placed in dialogue with the Louvre's Decorative Arts collection around light, colour, and sculpture.
exhibitions.note: Replaces the now-closed Michelangelo/Rodin show as the Louvre's summer pick

exhibitions.title: Ron Mueck
exhibitions.blurb: Eleven works tracing Mueck's uncanny hyperrealism, with the Japan premiere of 'Mass' — one hundred giant skulls — and six pieces never before shown in Japan.
exhibitions.note: Open to 22:00 most nights, 53rd floor

exhibitions.title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic
exhibitions.blurb: Historical works and new pieces tracing her enduring interest in ritual, eroticism, death, and the body as a site of political resistance.
exhibitions.note: One ticket covers all Gropius Bau shows; run extended a week to Aug 30

exhibitions.title: Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now
exhibitions.blurb: A comprehensive survey of one of Asia's defining artists — cyborgs, crashed utopias and mirrored futures — co-organised with Leeum after a 105,000-visitor run in Seoul.
exhibitions.note: West Gallery; closes early August

exhibitions.title: Design Ah! Experience the Wonder of Everyday Design
exhibitions.artist: Group show, based on the NHK TV program
exhibitions.blurb: Twenty-one interactive stations built around everyday actions, adapted for Hong Kong from the viral Japanese children's design TV program.
exhibitions.note: Good for visiting with kids

exhibitions.title: MMCA Collection: Korean Contemporary Art
exhibitions.blurb: The national collection rehung as a sweeping account of Korean contemporary art — the best single-building primer in the city.
exhibitions.note: On view into 2027

exhibitions.title: Objects of Glory: Iconic Moments in the History of Football
exhibitions.blurb: Sixteen legendary football objects, from Maradona's 1986 jersey to Pelé's 1970 boots, timed to World Cup 2026.
exhibitions.note: Pairs with Football & Art at the same museum

exhibitions.title: Football & Art: A Shared Emotion
exhibitions.blurb: Around one hundred works by sixty artists exploring football's cultural and emotional dimensions ahead of World Cup 2026.
exhibitions.note: Closes this weekend

exhibitions.title: Before the Eclipse: Archaeologies of Art in Mexico
exhibitions.blurb: More than sixty artists reassess 1980s Mexico through pre-Hispanic heritage, territory, and the country's globalizing turn.
exhibitions.note: Chapultepec Park

exhibitions.title: Carolina Caycedo: Confluências
exhibitions.blurb: The Colombian-American artist's work on rivers, extraction, and collective resistance, freshly opened on Avenida Paulista.
exhibitions.note: Includes a live performance, Atarraya, staged in the museum's Free Space

exhibitions.title: Pascale Marthine Tayou: Knockout! (Nocaute)
exhibitions.blurb: The Cameroonian artist's first institutional show in Brazil occupies all seven Pina Luz galleries.
exhibitions.note: Closes early August; free entry Saturdays and second Sunday of the month

exhibitions.title: Biennale Arte 2026: In Minor Keys
exhibitions.blurb: The 61st International Art Exhibition realises the late Koyo Kouoh's 'In Minor Keys' — 110 invited participants plus a hundred national pavilions across the city and Forte Marghera.
exhibitions.note: Allow two full days; comfortable shoes
